allSkills.name: High-Quality Synthesis (Quality Cap: 100)
allSkills.why: One of your earliest and strongest upgrades. Raising your quality ceiling makes every future synthesis more effective, so this should be among the first things you unlock.

allSkills.name: Conserve Materials
allSkills.why: A practical boost for field crafting and Simple Synthesis. This keeps your material usage efficient while you are still building up a larger stockpile.

allSkills.name: Gathering
allSkills.why: Improves the quality of gathered ingredients. Better inputs lead directly to better synthesis results, so this is a strong early investment.

allSkills.name: Gathering Technique
allSkills.why: Raises the yield from gather nodes. More ingredients per trip means faster progression and fewer return runs.

allSkills.name: Marksmanship
allSkills.why: Improves what you get from shooting gather points. It pairs well with other gathering upgrades and helps your exploration loop stay efficient.

allSkills.name: Item Rebuilding
allSkills.why: A valuable progression tool that lets you revisit crafted items and improve them further. It is also widely used in community SP farming routes.

allSkills.name: Particle Gathering
allSkills.why: Particles are used to unlock and level recipes at the Recipe Recall Station, so this is a key support node for your crafting progression.

allSkills.name: Quality Control
allSkills.why: A useful follow-up to the earlier quality upgrades. Expanding your quality ceiling gives more room to optimize each synthesis and improves output across the board.

allSkills.name: Synthesis Skill (×4, story-gated)
allSkills.why: New synthesis-related nodes can open up after major story progress, so it is worth checking the skill tree after key boss clears.

allSkills.name: High-Quality Synthesis (Quality Cap: 300)
allSkills.why: As enemies and recipes scale up, raising your quality ceiling becomes increasingly important for maintaining strong item performance.

allSkills.name: Firearm Accuracy
allSkills.why: A useful mid-game upgrade for shooting-based gathering. It is especially valuable if exploration is still a major source of materials for you.

allSkills.name: Friend Action
allSkills.why: A nice combat option once your core crafting and gathering loop is established. It adds value without demanding early priority.

allSkills.name: Keen Eye
allSkills.why: Helpful for targeted farming and rare drops, but not something you need to rush in the opening stretch of the game.

allSkills.name: High-Quality Synthesis (Quality Cap: 500 → 700 → 999)
allSkills.why: A late-game refinement that matters most once your crafting system is fully online. This is where your endgame synthesis starts to come together.
